title: Cast of incorrect Date is allowed in DuckDB instead of throwing exception
description:
### What happens?

DuckDB allows casting of incorrect Date field instead of throwing an Exception. 

### To Reproduce

```
CREATE TABLE TEST_DATA_2  AS
SELECT    '2020-01-01XXXX' AS DATE3
;

SELECT CAST(DATE3 AS DATE), DATE3 FROM TEST_DATA_2;
```

### OS:

x86_64

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.1

### DuckDB Client:

Python

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Yash

### Affiliation:

Student

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE TEST_DATA_1 (ID INTEGER, DATE_FIELD VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE TEST_DATA_2 AS SELECT '2020-01-01XXXX' AS DATE3;
SELECT CAST(DATE3 AS xsd:date) AS CASTED_DATE, DATE3 FROM TEST_DATA_2;
SELECT ID, CAST(DATE_FIELD AS xsd:date) FROM TEST_DATA_1 WHERE ID = 1;
key_bug_features: Incorrect Date casting allowed without exception
